content_markdown:
The weight of an object of mass $1\text{ kg}$ at the surface of Mars is about $4\text{ N}$. The radius of Mars is about half the radius of Earth. Which of the following is the best estimate of the ratio below?

$$\frac{\text{mass of Mars}}{\text{mass of Earth}}$$

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present, only text and a mathematical fraction.

vibe_explanation: Weight is $W = mg = G \frac{Mm}{r^2}$. For a $1\text{ kg}$ mass, $g = G \frac{M}{r^2}$. On Earth, $g_E \approx 10\text{ m s}^{-2}$. On Mars, $g_M = 4\text{ m s}^{-2}$. Using the ratio $\frac{g_M}{g_E} = \frac{M_M}{M_E} \cdot (\frac{r_E}{r_M})^2$, we substitute the knowns: $\frac{4}{10} = \frac{M_M}{M_E} \cdot (2)^2$. This simplifies to $0.4 = \frac{M_M}{M_E} \cdot 4$, so the ratio is $0.1$.
